originalTitle: Sudan drone attack on key hospital killed 64 people during Eid, WHO says
title: A Sad Day at the Hospital in Sudan
content:
Hello, brave young friends! Your friend, the kid-friendly news reporter, is here to share some important news from our big continent, Africa. Today, we're talking about a country called Sudan. It's a very big country, full of wonderful people and places, just like where you live.

A Hospital is Hurt

Sadly, we have some news that makes our hearts feel a little heavy. In Sudan, something very sad happened at a hospital. Do you know what a hospital is for? It's a very special place where kind doctors and nurses help people who are sick or hurt get better. Hospitals are places of hope and healing, where everyone should always feel safe.

What Happened?

During a special happy holiday called Eid, a flying robot, which some people call a drone, hit a hospital. It made a lot of noise and caused a lot of damage. This is very sad because hospitals are meant to be safe places, even when grown-ups are having disagreements or fighting. When this flying robot hit, many people inside the hospital were hurt.

Who Was There?

Imagine being in a hospital to get better, or working there to help others. That's where these people were. The World Health Organization, which is a big group of grown-ups who help people stay healthy all over the world, told us about it. They said that 64 people, including little children, kind nurses, and a smart doctor, were hurt very badly and sadly, they are no longer with us. This is a very sad thing to hear, especially when children are involved.

What is a Hospital For?

A hospital is like a superhero headquarters for health! When you fall and scrape your knee, or when you have a fever, your parents might take you to a clinic or a hospital. The doctors listen to your heart, give you medicine, or put a bandage on your hurt. Nurses give you kind smiles and make sure you are comfortable. Hospitals are there to make us feel better and safe. They should never, ever be hurt. Did you know? The first hospitals were built a very, very long time ago to help travelers and people who were sick on their journeys! They have always been places for healing and care.

What is Eid?

Eid is a very special and happy holiday for many people around the world, including in Sudan. It's a time for families to get together, share yummy food, wear new clothes, and give gifts. It’s like Christmas or Diwali or any big celebration day in your family! Everyone is usually very happy and celebrating. So, for something sad to happen during such a happy time makes it even more difficult for the people in Sudan. Did you know? There are two main Eid holidays each year! One is called Eid al-Fitr, and the other is Eid al-Adha. Both are times for joy, thankfulness, and thinking of others.

What is a Drone?

Sometimes, a drone is like a toy remote-control airplane or helicopter that you might see. They can fly high in the sky. Some drones are used for good things, like taking cool pictures, helping to deliver medicines to faraway places, or checking on farms. But sometimes, sadly, grown-ups use them for bad things, like causing harm. This is not what drones were made for, and it's very sad when they are used in a way that hurts people. Did you know? The very first drones were invented over 100 years ago, but they looked very different from the ones we see today!

Why Hospitals Must Be Safe

Imagine if you were feeling poorly and went to the doctor, but the hospital wasn't safe. That would be very scary, wouldn't it? That's why grown-ups all over the world have rules that say hospitals, doctors, and nurses must always be protected. They are there to help everyone, no matter what, and they should be able to do their important work safely. It’s a very important rule that helps keep all of us safe.

Who is Helping?

The World Health Organization (WHO), the big group we talked about, is working hard to tell everyone what happened. They want to remind people that hospitals should always be safe places. Many other kind people and groups are also helping the people in Sudan. They are trying to bring comfort, food, and more doctors to help everyone who is hurting. It shows that even when sad things happen, there are always good people trying to make things better. Did you know? The WHO has a special day every year, on April 7th, called World Health Day, to remind everyone how important it is to be healthy and to help others stay healthy too!

What Can We Do?

Even though we are far away, we can still do something. We can hope for peace in Sudan and all over Africa. We can try to be extra kind to our friends and family today. We can learn more about different countries and remember that we are all part of one big, human family. You can even draw a picture of a dove, which is a symbol of peace, to send a good thought to the children of Sudan. It's sad to hear about people getting hurt, especially in a hospital and during a happy holiday. But let's remember the doctors and nurses who are still working hard to help people, and hope that soon, all hospitals will be safe, peaceful places for everyone, everywhere.
summary: During a happy holiday called Eid, a flying robot hit a hospital in Sudan, hurting many people, including children, nurses, and a doctor.
whyMatters: This is important because hospitals are very special places where sick and hurt people go to get better, and they should always be safe for everyone.

originalTitle: Kenyans fighting illegally for Russia in Ukraine to be granted amnesty
title: Kenyans fighting illegally for Russia in Ukraine to be granted amnesty
content: It is illegal for Kenyans to serve in foreign armies and can carry a sentence of up to 10 years in jail.

originalTitle: 'Peace is a gradual thing': How land, cattle and identity fuel a deadly Nigerian conflict
title: Let's Learn About Peace in Nigeria!
content:
Hello, Super Friends!

Hello, amazing friends! It's your kid-friendly reporter from Africa here, ready to share some important news with you. Today, we're going to talk about a place in Nigeria called Plateau State. Sometimes, grown-ups there have disagreements. But don't worry, we'll talk about it in a way that's easy to understand, and we'll remember that peace is always possible!

Where is Plateau State?

Imagine a map of Africa. Nigeria is a big country right there! And inside Nigeria, there's a beautiful place called Plateau State. It's famous for its lovely hills, green farms, and lots of different people living there. Think of it like a giant village where many families live together. **Did you know?** Nigeria is the most populated country in Africa! That means lots and lots of people live there!

What's Happening There?

Sometimes, in Plateau State, people have big disagreements. These disagreements can sometimes turn into fights. When people fight, it's called conflict. It's like when you and your friend argue over a toy, but much bigger and more serious. People get sad, and it can be hard for everyone to live happily. We want everyone to be safe and happy, right? So, let's try to understand why these disagreements happen.

Who Owns the Land?

One big reason for disagreements is land. Land is super important! Farmers need land to grow yummy food like maize, yams, and vegetables. They need space for their homes too. If someone builds their house or plants their crops on land that someone else thinks is theirs, it can cause a problem. Imagine if you built a sandcastle, and someone else came and started building their own castle right on top of yours! You'd be upset, wouldn't you? It's kind of like that, but with farms and homes. **Did you know?** Land is not just for farming; it's also where our homes are built and where we play! Many important animals like elephants and giraffes also need land to live.

Cows and Green Grass

Another reason for disagreements is about cows and where they eat. In Plateau State, some people are herders. Herders take care of many cows, sheep, and goats. These animals need lots of green grass to eat so they can give us milk and meat. Herders move their animals around to find the best places for them to graze. But sometimes, the herders' cows walk onto the farmers' land and eat their crops! This makes the farmers very sad and angry because their food is ruined. And the herders need food for their animals. So, finding enough land for both farming and grazing is a big puzzle. **Did you know?** Cows are super important animals! They give us milk for our cereal and cheese, and sometimes even leather for our shoes! They help many families earn a living.

Different Families, Different Ways

Plateau State has many different groups of people, almost like different big families or tribes. Each group has its own special ways of talking, cooking, and celebrating. This is called their identity. It's beautiful to have so many different ways! But sometimes, people from different groups don't understand each other very well. They might think their way is the only right way. This can lead to misunderstandings and make the disagreements bigger. It's important to remember that everyone is special, no matter their group, and we can all learn from each other. **Did you know?** Africa is home to thousands of different languages and cultures! Isn't that amazing? Imagine all the different stories and songs!

When There's No Justice

When something bad happens, like crops being ruined or someone getting hurt, we expect someone to help make things right. This is called justice. It means making sure fairness happens and that people who do wrong things learn from their mistakes. It's like when a teacher helps you and your friend share a toy fairly. But sometimes, people in Plateau State feel like justice doesn't happen quickly enough, or that it's not fair. If people feel like no one is helping them make things right, they might try to solve problems themselves, which can sometimes make the disagreements even worse. This is why good grown-up helpers are so important.

Trusting Our Helpers

Just like we trust our teachers and parents to help us, grown-ups trust people like the police and soldiers to keep everyone safe. These people are called security forces. They are supposed to help stop fights and make sure everyone follows the rules. Their job is to protect everyone. However, sometimes people in Plateau State feel like these helpers don't always come fast enough, or that they don't always understand the problem well. When people don't fully trust the helpers, they might not feel safe, and it becomes harder to make peace. We need our helpers to be strong and fair for everyone, listening to all sides of a story.

Why Peace is So Important

Peace means living together happily, sharing, and helping each other. When there is peace, children can go to school every day, farmers can grow food without worry, and herders can take care of their animals safely. Everyone can smile and laugh more! Imagine a world where everyone is kind and helpful. Without peace, it's hard to learn, hard to play, and hard to grow. Homes can be broken, and friends can be lost. That's why working for peace, even when it's tricky, is the most important job for everyone. Peace brings joy and allows communities to build wonderful things together.

What Can We Do?

Even as kids, you can help! How? By being kind to everyone, even if they are different from you. By listening to your friends and trying to understand their feelings. By sharing your toys and snacks. By talking about problems instead of shouting. These small things make a big difference, like planting tiny seeds of friendship! Remember what the original news said: "Peace is a gradual thing." That means it takes time, like planting a seed and watching it grow into a strong tree. Every little act of kindness is like watering that seed, making peace grow stronger and stronger.

Let's Hope for a Brighter Tomorrow!

So, my friends, while there are sometimes problems in places like Plateau State, we must always remember that people are working hard for peace. By understanding why these problems happen, we can all wish for a brighter, more peaceful tomorrow for everyone in Africa and around the world. Let's send our happy thoughts and wishes for peace to Plateau State! Together, we can hope for a future full of smiles and shared joys.
summary: In Nigeria's Plateau State, people sometimes disagree and fight over land for farming and cows, and because they are from different groups, which is made harder when justice is slow and people don't fully trust the helpers, but working for peace is very important so everyone can live happily and safely.
whyMatters: It matters because when people fight, nobody is safe or happy, and children cannot go to school or play; peace helps everyone live better lives, grow food, and build a strong future together, allowing communities to flourish and dreams to come true.
